content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-autonomous agent framework) can be repurposed to prioritize the retention of new memory entries in state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents, where only memory traces that trigger high intrinsic curiosity are kept to optimize limited context window capacity. This creates a self-sustaining memory pruning loop where an agent's own desire to explore unknown patterns drives what it remembers for future active learning iterations.
